form-instructions.married: To estimate your benefits, we will need the following information from your and your partner's {{year}} tax returns.
form-instructions.single: To estimate your benefits, we will need the following information from your {{year}} tax return.
form-instructions.note: If any total amounts are negative or zero, enter '0'.

info.claimed-income: To calculate this amount, add line 11700 and line 12500 of your {{year}} tax return.
info.claimed-repayment: To calculate this amount, add line 21300 and line 23200 of your {{year}} tax return.
info.net-income: This is found on line 23600 of your {{year}} tax return.
info.partner-claimed-income: To calculate this amount, add line 11700 and line 12500 of your partner's {{year}} tax return.
info.partner-claimed-repayment: To calculate this amount, add line 21300 and line 23200 of your partner's {{year}} tax return.
info.partner-net-income: This is found on line 23600 of your partner's {{year}} tax return.

items: Taxable scholarships, fellowships, bursaries and artists' project grants (line 13010)
Business income (use 0 if the amount is negative) (line 13500)
Professional income (use 0 if the amount is negative) (line 13700)
Commission income (use 0 if the amount is negative) (line 13900)
Farming income (use 0 if the amount is negative) (line 14100)
Fishing income (use 0 if the amount is negative) (line 14300)
working-income.text1: To calculate your working income, add up all the following lines from your {{year}} tax return:
working-income.description: This is income you made from your employment or self-employment.

partner-working-income.text1: To calculate your partner's working income, add up all the following lines from their {{year}} tax return:
partner-working-income.description: This is income your partner made from their employment or self-employment.

content.completion-time: It will take about 5 to 10 minutes to complete.
content.description: Use this tool to estimate how much money you could get from the Canada Disability Benefit. This is not an application.

estimator-steps.intro: You will need to answer questions about your:

content.result-disclaimer: This tool does check if you are eligible. It only gives an estimate of the amount you may get if you are eligible. You can check the requirements at Canada.ca/disability-benefit.

results.contact: For a more accurate assessment of your estimated benefits amount, please <cdbContactLink>contact us</cdbContactLink>.
results.description: This tool shows an estimate of monthly payments from {{paymentPeriodStart}} and {{paymentPeriodEnd}}. Amounts may change each year due to inflation (cost of living).

common-law.text: You live with a partner as a couple but are not married to them.
common-law.p1: At least one of these apply:
common-law.item1: You've lived together for at least 12 continuous months. This includes times you were separated for less than 90 days because of relationship problems.
common-law.item2: You share a child (by birth or adoption).
common-law.item3: Your partner has custody and control of your child right before they turned 19 and your child depends on them for support.

divorced.text: You were married and are now legally divorced.

separated.text: You've been living apart from your spouse or common-law partner for at least 90 days because your relationship ended.
separated.p1: Once you have been separated for 90 days, the separation is the date you started living apart.
separated.p2: You're not considered separated if any of these apply:
separated.item1: You're living apart for reasons such as work, school or health.
separated.item2: Your spouse or common-law partner is in jail.
separated.item3: Your spouse or common-law partner does not live in Canada.

single.text: You're single. None of the other statuses apply to you.

widowed.text: Your spouse or common-law partner has died.

next-steps.apply-cdb: Apply for the Canada Disability Benefit
next-steps.learn-more: Learn more about the Canada Disability Benefit

married-common-law.cdb-partner-result: If both you and your partner are receiving the Canada Disability Benefit, you could receive a monthly amount of {{result}}.
married-common-law.intro: The monthly benefit amount you could receive depends on whether your partner is receiving the Canada Disability Benefit.
married-common-law.non-cdb-partner-result: If your partner is not receiving the Canada Disability Benefit, you could receive a monthly amount of {{result}}.

your-estimate.note: Please note that you may be eligible to get back payments for up to 24 months from the date we receive your application. There will be no back payments prior to July 2025.

single.intro: You could be eligible to receive:
single.result: A monthly amount of {{result}} from the Canada Disability Benefit.

results.disclaimer-alert: This is only an estimate based on the information you provided.
